chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Bail App. No. S – 88 of 2025 Cr. Bail App. No. S – 89 of 2025 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of pre-arrest bail 1. For orders on office objections at Flag-A 2. For hearing of pre-arrest bail 12.01.2026 M/s Muhammad Bux Bangwar and Muhammad Ali Napar, Advocates for applicant. Mr. Imran Mobeen Khan, Assistant Prosecutor General..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- Applicant is present on interim pre-arrest bail. Learned Counsel for the applicant in Cr. Bail App. No. S-89 of 2025 submits a statement; according to which, the ‘C’ class recommendation of the FIR has been approved and certified copy of the order thereof is annexed with the statement. He requires the said Cr. Bail App. No. S-89 of 2025 to be disposed of as not pressed. Ordered accordingly. As to Cr. Bail App. No. S-88 of 2025, learned Counsel has submitted a statement along with photocopies of orders passed by the learned Additional Sessions Judge-IV (Hudood), Sukkur, dated 04.08.2021, 10.09.2021 and 11.06.2022, further stating that no order of taking of cognizance is available therein. He further refers to page 99, a letter dated 10th July, 2021, as written by the Prosecutor General, Criminal Prosecution Services Department, Government of Sindh, contending that the ‘B’ class report in the subject FIR has been referred therein. Learned Assistant P.G., however, states that no order of the acceptance of the report is available. Irrespective to the same, once a report having been submitted, the applicant apparently has made out a case of further enquiry. The interim order dated 03.02.2025, passed in Cr. Bail App. No. S-88 of 2025, stands confirmed.
No. S-88 of 2025, stands confirmed. Both these matters stand disposed of accordingly. Office to place a signed copy of this order in the captioned connected matter. J U D G E Abdul Basit
